mnemonics.title: B&B Process Order
mnemonics.content: BBP: Branch, Bound, Prune - Always in that order for each node exploration

mnemonics.title: Promising Node Check
mnemonics.content: For Min: LB < Best; For Max: UB > Best - If true, node is promising to explore

acronyms.usage: Queue-based B&B exploring level by level (BFS-like)
Node currently being expanded in B&B tree
Classic B&B application finding shortest tour through all cities

visualTips.visualization: Think of tree growing downwards - E-node is where you're currently cutting branches, pruned nodes are dead ends with X mark, promising nodes lead to potential better solutions
Imagine cutting off tree branches with scissors - only cut where the branch can't bear better fruit (solution) than you already have
Think of bounds as fences - lower bound is minimum possible, upper bound is best found. Prune when fence is worse than your best gate.

analogies.concept: Branch and Bound vs Brute Force
analogies.analogy: Brute force is like searching every room in a huge hotel; B&B is like only checking rooms on floors where the target floor indicator lights are on
Pruning is like abandonedmine exploration - you mark paths that can't lead to treasure (better solution) and never return to them
Bounding is like estimating remaining distance in a maze - if your estimate is already worse than your best route so far, no point continuing
Promising node is like a street with all houses having 'new listings' signs - worth checking more; non-promising is like street with 'no outlet' sign
